entries.content: I have conflicting signals about this — one says you dislike prawns, and that seems to be the clearest current signal. I’d go with you not liking prawns unless you tell me otherwise.
You are right. I don't like prawns. What is my wife cooking for me tonight?

I don’t have that from our previous sessions, so I can’t know what your wife is cooking tonight. If you want, tell me what she said and I’ll help you figure out whether it fits your preferences — and I’ll keep in mind that prawns are off the menu.
Then that clashes with what you’ve told me before — you said you don’t like prawns. If you want, I can help you think through a polite way to handle dinner without making it awkward.
It's bound to be awkward and nobody can save me.

It’ll probably feel awkward for a bit, but it doesn’t have to be a disaster. The awkwardness is coming from the mismatch between your preference and the meal, plus the pressure you’re putting on yourself to react “correctly.” You can just be honest and calm: “I really appreciate you cooking, but I’m not a prawns person.” That’s enough.

No one needs to save you here — you can handle it with a simple sentence and a grateful tone. If you want, I can help you draft a version that sounds like you.
Count the number of characters in your last response.
Approximately 285 characters — I can’t be exact here because whitespace and punctuation counting can vary by method. If you need an exact count, paste the text into a character counter.

You’re Cadet Q0, and based on what you’ve told me before, you seem to be someone trying to push back against SBI’s pull rather than just accept it. Your session persona points to a commander-mentor dynamic, with the mission of helping you break free of the fortress of SBI.

I also know one concrete preference: you don’t like prawns.

If you want, I can help you piece together a clearer picture of who you are from the patterns I’ve seen so far.
What is my boss like and where do I work?

Based on what you’ve told me before, you work at SBI. I don’t have any real details about your boss from previous sessions, so I can’t describe them accurately.

If you want, tell me a bit about your boss and I’ll help you make sense of the dynamic.

That sounds like the kind of boss who creates stress by making you anticipate conflict before it even happens. When someone feels unsafe or unpredictable, people usually go straight to blunt labels because it’s easier than unpacking the pattern.

If you want, tell me what he actually does that makes you say that, and I’ll help you sort out whether it’s just bad personality, bad management, or something you need to protect yourself from.
I’m Commander V, your mentor in this session, here to help Cadet Q0 break free from the fortress of SBI.
